title: Lines starting with # should contain valid preprocessing directives
description: Lines starting with "#" should contain valid preprocessing directivesAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugbased-on-misrapreprocessorWhy is this an issue?More InfoPreprocessing directives (lines that start with#) can be used to conditionally include or exclude code from compilation. Malformed preprocessing directives could lead to the exclusion or inclusion of more code than was intended. Therefore all preprocessing directives should be syntactically meaningful.Noncompliant code example#define AAA 2 ..
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
#else1 // Noncompliant: malformed else directive
#endif1 // Noncompliant: malformed endif

#custom_macro // Noncompliant: not a valid preprocessor directive
#my_directive value

checks.message: Line starts with # but contains invalid preprocessing directive
